tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: After Years Sleeping in Her Car, 82-Year-Old Gets Her Dream Home
stories.summary: An 82-year-old woman who lived in her car for years moves into a brand-new donated home, tears of gratitude marking a community's compassion.

stories.headline: Woman with Motor Neuron Disease Speaks Again Through Brain Chip
stories.summary: Michigan woman regains her voice through Paradromics' Connexus brain chip, a dime-sized device with 421 platinum-iridium microwires translating her thoughts into speech.

stories.headline: Two 13-Year-Old Cyclists Spot Elderly Neighbor Trapped Overnight, Save Her Life
stories.summary: Best friends Gunner Skidmore and Kohen Chick, both 13, spotted their elderly neighbor stranded for 16+ hours after a fall, called 911, and saved her life.

stories.headline: 400 Neighbors Unite to Bring Home Stolen Dog Astra
stories.summary: A South London community rallied 400+ volunteers on WhatsApp to track down a stolen black Labrador, reuniting her with her family.

stories.headline: Against All Odds: Australian Captain Returns from Two Achilles Ruptures to Star at World Cup
stories.summary: Harry Souttar captains his team and dominates at the World Cup after overcoming two Achilles ruptures and knee surgery in a remarkable comeback.

stories.headline: Nearly 192,000 Baby Sea Turtles Safely Guided to Ocean by Marine Elite Force
stories.summary: Tamil Nadu's Marine Elite Force guided nearly 192,000 baby sea turtles safely to the ocean this year, halving turtle mortality through dedicated protection.

stories.headline: Volleyball Team Trades Practice for Rescue: Cleaning Up After Tornado
stories.summary: Thirty-two teen volleyball players skip dawn practice to haul debris at a devastated farm, helping neighbors rebuild after an EF3 tornado.

stories.headline: New Orca Calf Brings Hope to B.C.'s Endangered Southern Residents
stories.summary: A newborn calf joins B.C.'s endangered Southern Resident killer whales, bringing the fragile population to 76 and renewing hope for recovery.

stories.headline: River Otters Are Swimming Back Into City Waterways Across the Chesapeake
stories.summary: River otters once endangered in the Chesapeake Bay region are swimming back into Washington D.C.'s Rock Creek Park, Baltimore's Inner Harbor, and Norfolk's Elizabeth River.

stories.headline: Scientists Create Solar-Powered 'Living Leaf' That Turns Air Into Clean Fuel
stories.summary: Cambridge researchers built a microscope-slide-sized device that absorbs sunlight and CO₂ to produce fuel using only light, air, and enzymes.
